## vvx
Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Docket:
  ## Character
  Mechanical migration with a real axis-split at the seam: one bare `RBCC_role_*`
  family that today serves three jobs gets cleaved into a minted enum family and a
  bare composition family. Edits are localized and value-preserving on the bare side;
  the judgment is resolved below. Imminent execution — every file:line below was
  verified against the tree at slate time, and the operator has frozen this surface
  (no commits land here before mount), so the refs are trustworthy as written.
  
  ## Goal
  Make the RBRA-role enum greppable as a sprue family (`rbnae_*`) WITHOUT touching any
  Google Cloud service-account email, account-id, or local secret-directory name. A
  deliberate partial cleanup: mint only the enum-value axis; leave every derived
  resource-name string bare.
  
  ## The decision (locked by operator) — `{role, account}` split, Design A
  
  The current single family `RBCC_role_*` conflates two axes:
  - **Enum-value axis** — the `RBRA_ROLE` value: written into credential files, validated,
    carried as the auth-regime folio, swizzle-checked. Domain = {governor, retriever,
    director} only.
  - **Composition-label axis** — bare fragments that build GCP SA account-ids/emails AND
    local secret-directory names. Domain = {governor, retriever, director, payor, assay,
    mason}.
  
  Cleave into two `RBCC_` families (leading-space comments so no line opens with hash):
  
  ```
    RBCC_role_governor="rbnae_governor"      # minted enum — RBRA_ROLE domain (3 members)
    RBCC_role_retriever="rbnae_retriever"
    RBCC_role_director="rbnae_director"
  
    RBCC_account_governor="governor"         # bare composition — SA-name + secret-dir (6 members)
    RBCC_account_retriever="retriever"
    RBCC_account_director="director"
    RBCC_account_payor="payor"
    RBCC_account_assay="assay"
    RBCC_account_mason="mason"
  ```
  
  `assay`/`mason`/`payor` are NEVER `RBRA_ROLE` values (validation accepts only
  governor|retriever|director), so they get NO `rbnae_` token — `account`-only.
  
  **Design A — secret directories stay bare** (operator-approved): a secret-dir name is a
  derived resource-name string in local-filesystem space, structurally identical to the SA
  email and the `-tether`/`-airgap` pool suffixes the heat already keeps bare under class-4.
  So directories follow `RBCC_account_*`; they are NOT renamed. The folio→file `case` in
  `zrbra_resolve_role` is the translation point that lets the folio mint while the directory
  stays bare. The prior "operators rename dirs by hand" / "have dirs been renamed?" breadcrumb
  and the flat→nested migration loop are VOID — retire them (section D).
  
  ## Why the split is safe (already-shaped seams — verified)
  - `zrbgg_create_service_account_with_key(account_name, …, role)` already takes
    account-name ($1, builds the email) and role ($4, writes RBRA_ROLE) as separate params.
    Split = feed $1 from `RBCC_account_*`, $4 from `RBCC_role_*`.
  - `zrbra_resolve_role` (rbra_cli.sh) maps folio → `RBDC_*_RBRA_FILE` *variable* via a
    `case`, so the folio string is decoupled from the directory name.
  - Swizzle guard (rbra_cli.sh) ties only `RBRA_ROLE == BUZ_FOLIO`; both mint together.
  
  ## Edit map — by axis
  All file:line refs verified against the tree at slate time.
  
  ### A. Declarations — rbcc_Constants.sh
  - :59-64 — replace the 6-member `RBCC_role_*` block with the two families above.
  - :94-95 — `RBCC_fact_ext_roster_*` compose fact-FILE names → use `RBCC_account_*`
    (keeps fact filenames `roster-retriever` bare; minting here would corrupt filenames).
  - :101 — rewrite the "Distinct from the credential RBCC_role_* family" axis comment to
    describe the role(enum)/account(label) split.
  
  ### B. Minted (enum-value axis → `RBCC_role_*` = `rbnae_*`)
  - rbgg_Governor.sh:592, :643 — invest $4 (becomes RBRA_ROLE).
  - rbgp_Payor.sh:1876 — `printf 'RBRA_ROLE=%s' "${RBCC_role_governor}"`.
  - rbra_regime.sh:69-71 — validation `case` arms → `rbnae_governor|rbnae_retriever|rbnae_director)`.
  - rbra_regime.sh:41 — enroll help string text → new values (each ≤20 chars; `rbnae_retriever`
    is 15, within `buv_string_enroll RBRA_ROLE 1 20`).
  - rbra_cli.sh:39-44 — `zrbra_resolve_role` case arms → `rbnae_*)`; RBDC var targets unchanged.
  - rbra_cli.sh:83 — `z_roles=(...)` display array → `rbnae_*` (labels the enum).
  - rbra_cli.sh:35,43 — "role required / Unknown role" die-text → reflect minted values.
  - **Tools/buk/buts/butcrg_RegimeCredentials.sh:36,48-49** — `z_roles=("governor"
    "retriever" "director")` is passed as the **folio** to `rbw-rav`/`rbw-rar` (:48-49).
    The folio mints, so `z_roles` must become `rbnae_*` or `zrbra_resolve_role` dies
    "Unknown RBRA role". CAUTION: this site is caught by NONE of the build/fast/grep
    gates — it is a workstation-only credential test, and the grep audit reads its bare
    `governor` as an acceptable class-C survivor. Silent breakage; this edit plus the
    required butcrg run (acceptance gate) are the only things that catch it.
  
  ### C. Bare (composition-label axis → `RBCC_account_*`)
  - rbgg_Governor.sh:560,569 — roster first arg (SA-name prefix match).
  - rbgg_Governor.sh:578,583,629,634,825,830,839,844 — `z_account_name`, divest first arg,
    and the "composes X-<identity>" doc strings.
  - rbgd_DepotConstants.sh:61 — `RBGD_MASON_EMAIL`.
  - rbgp_Payor.sh:1226,1622 — mason name/email; :1412,1744 — `== ${RBCC_role_governor}-*`
    SA-email match; :1772 — governor account-id.
  - Secret-dir paths (Design A, bare): rbdc_DerivedConstants.sh:35-39 (mkdir), :74-78
    (`RBDC_*_RBRA_FILE`); rblm_cli.sh:88,125,167; rbho path-checks rbhodb_director_bind.sh:49,
    rbhoda_director_airgap.sh:52, rbhodf_director_first_build.sh:40, rbhodg_director_graft.sh:51.
  
  ### C-bis. Explicitly left bare — NO edit (judgment recorded so the executor need not re-derive)
  - `rbgv_AccessProbe.sh` `zrbgv_role_rbra_file_capture` (~:76-95) has its OWN
    `governor)|director)|retriever)` role→file `case` plus internal bare labels in
    `rbgv_check_*`. These are bare-axis (file resolution + internal labels, structurally
    the directory) and STAY bare. They work post-split only because the probe sources the
    RBRA file and runs `zrbra_enforce`, whose case is minted in section B — confirm that
    coupling holds, then leave rbgv untouched.
  - Pre-existing bare `governor-*` literals NOT sourced from `RBCC_role_*`, all intentionally
    bare and untouched: rbgp_Payor.sh:1687 (doc prose), :1712 (step message), :1811
    (`"governor-owner"` IAM request-id label). The grep audit will surface these — leave them.
  
  ### D. Retire (no-auto-repair posture; Design A voids these)
  - rbdc_DerivedConstants.sh:42-71 — delete the flat→nested one-shot migration (both the
    role loop :42-61 and the payor `rbro.env` block :63-71). It is dead auto-repair.
    Post-split it goes INCOHERENT, not merely stale: :44/:46 build the dir path from
    `RBCC_role_*` (now `rbnae_governor/…`) while the real secret dirs stay bare — a path
    mismatch — so it cannot be mechanically repointed. Delete, do not repair. Drop the
    rename breadcrumb entirely — under Design A nothing renames.
  
  ### E. The one mixed-arg seam — split `zrbho_credential_install`
  - rbhob_base.sh:88 `zrbho_credential_install(z_role_constant)` uses its single arg as BOTH
    the displayed dir path (:97, :115 — needs bare) AND the folio passed to `rbw-rav`
    (:135 — needs minted). Give it two params: `(z_account_label, z_role_folio)`.
    - :97,:115 dir-path → `z_account_label` (bare).
    - :135 `buh_tt … "${RBZ_VALIDATE_AUTH}" "" " ${z_role_folio}"` → minted folio.
    - Callers: rbhocr_credential_retriever.sh:38, rbhocd_credential_director.sh:38 — pass
      `"${RBCC_account_X}" "${RBCC_role_X}"`.
  
  ### F. Rust projection + consumers (value-preserving rename)
  - rbcc_Constants.sh:126-134 — the `rbcc_emit_consts` projection list currently emits
    `RBCC_role_*` (6) → `RBTDGC_ROLE_*`. Rust uses these ONLY as secret-dir names
    (rbtdrp_pristine.rs:288 `secrets.join(role)`), i.e. the bare axis. Repoint the list to
    the 6 `RBCC_account_*`; projected names become `RBTDGC_ACCOUNT_*` (values unchanged).
  - rbcc_Constants.sh:110-119 — the projection-block comment ("credential roles", "the
    manifest role mirror") describes the projected set; refresh that prose to "account
    labels" so it matches the repointed list.
  - Discovery: `grep -rn 'RBTDGC_ROLE_' Tools/rbk/rbtd/src` — repoint every hit
    (rbtdrp_pristine `RBTDRP_RBRA_ROLES` = GOVERNOR/DIRECTOR/RETRIEVER/ASSAY, plus any
    rbtdrk/rbtdtm_manifest/lib.rs mirror) to `RBTDGC_ACCOUNT_*`.
  - Also `grep -rn 'RBRA_ROLE' Tools/rbk/rbtd/src` — IF any Rust fixture asserts an RBRA_ROLE
    *value* (not a dir path), it needs the minted value; then ALSO project `RBCC_role_*` as
    `RBTDGC_ROLE_*` and point only those value-assertions at it. (Verified at slate time:
    none — current Rust role use is `secrets.join(role)` dir-path only.)
  - Rebuild: `tt/vow-b.Build.sh`; unit tests: `tt/rbw-tt.Test.sh`.
  
  ### G. Spec text
  - BUS0 (Tools/buk/vov_veiled/BUS0-BashUtilitiesSpec.adoc) "Target Enums" (~:1025-1040) —
    add `Auth regime (rbnae_): RBRA_ROLE — governor/retriever/director → rbnae_*`, and a one-
    line note that auth-role uniquely splits into a minted enum family (`RBCC_role_*`,
    RBRA_ROLE domain) and a bare composition family (`RBCC_account_*`, SA-names + secret
    dirs), the bare side justified by class-4.
  - adoc account-name prose carrying `${RBCC_role_*}-` (RBSRL-retriever_roster:39,48,
    RBSRK-retriever_invest:15,33, RBSDK-director_invest:12,30, RBSDR-director_roster:37,45) —
    these describe the SA `accountId` prefix → switch the attribute ref to `RBCC_account_*`.
  
  ## Critical test caveat — the enum gate does NOT cover roles
  `RBRA_ROLE` is `buv_string_enroll` + a hand-written `case`, NOT `buv_enum_enroll`. So the
  paddock's "regime-validation rejects a bare survivor once the declaration flips" safety net
  does NOT apply here. The per-family grep audit AND the required butcrg run (below) are
  therefore LOAD-BEARING, not confirmatory — butcrg is the only automated exercise of the
  minted folio through `rbw-rav`/`rbw-rar`.
  
  ## Acceptance gate
  - `tt/vow-b.Build.sh` clean (deny-warnings) + `tt/rbw-tt.Test.sh` green.
  - `tt/rbw-ts.TestSuite.fast.sh` green (read ../logs-buk/last.txt; never pipe the tabtarget).
  - shellcheck via release-qualify path if touched broadly.
  - Grep audit: `grep -rn 'rbnae_governor\|rbnae_retriever\|rbnae_director' Tools/` returns a
    coherent enum-value cluster (RBRA_ROLE writes/validation/folio/resolver/display + the
    butcrg test folio only); NO `rbnae_*` leaks into an SA-name, email, or directory path.
    Bare `governor`/`director`/`retriever` survivors must all be: class-3 (colophons rbw-ac*,
    function names, doc filenames), rbgv resolver labels (C-bis), the rbgp prose/label
    literals (C-bis: :1687/:1712/:1811), or class-C account/dir composition.
  - **Required credential proof (replaces the former "optional" leg) — the mint is only
    proven end-to-end against real credentials, so close this gap rather than waving it:**
    - **Workstation, always:** run the `butcrg_RegimeCredentials.sh` RBRA cases (find its
      enrolled dispatch) — green proves the minted folio resolves through `rbw-rav`/`rbw-rar`
      and the swizzle guard. Also run `rbw-rav rbnae_<role>` for each role whose RBRA file is
      present.
    - **If GCP creds present:** a fresh invest (`rbw-aM` governor / `rbw-arI` retriever /
      `rbw-adI` director) writes `RBRA_ROLE=rbnae_*`, then the matching access-probe
      (`rbw-acg`/`rbw-acr`/`rbw-acd`) sources it and reaches GCP — exercising rbgv resolution
      + `zrbra_enforce` against the minted value.
    - **If creds for any role are absent:** state so explicitly in the wrap summary — name the
      untested leg. No silent skip.
  
  ## What done looks like
  Two `RBCC_` families exist; `RBRA_ROLE`/folio/validation/resolver/butcrg-test carry `rbnae_*`;
  every SA email/account-id and every secret-directory name is byte-identical to before; the
  flat→nested migration loop and rename breadcrumb are gone; rbgv and the rbgp prose/label
  literals left bare by design; BUS0 lists `rbnae_`; Rust projects `RBTDGC_ACCOUNT_*` and
  builds/tests green; the required credential proof is run (or its absent legs named in the wrap).

Docket:
  ## Character
  Mechanical migration with a real axis-split at the seam: one bare `RBCC_role_*`
  family that today serves three jobs gets cleaved into a minted enum family and a
  bare composition family. Edits are localized and value-preserving on the bare side;
  the judgment is resolved below. Imminent execution — every file:line below was
  verified against the tree at slate time, and the operator has frozen this surface
  (no commits land here before mount), so the refs are trustworthy as written.
  
  ## Goal
  Make the RBRA-role enum greppable as a sprue family (`rbnae_*`) WITHOUT touching any
  Google Cloud service-account email, account-id, or local secret-directory name. A
  deliberate partial cleanup: mint only the enum-value axis; leave every derived
  resource-name string bare.
  
  ## The decision (locked by operator) — `{role, account}` split, Design A
  
  The current single family `RBCC_role_*` conflates two axes:
  - **Enum-value axis** — the `RBRA_ROLE` value: written into credential files, validated,
    carried as the auth-regime folio, swizzle-checked. Domain = {governor, retriever,
    director} only.
  - **Composition-label axis** — bare fragments that build GCP SA account-ids/emails AND
    local secret-directory names. Domain = {governor, retriever, director, payor, assay,
    mason}.
  
  Cleave into two `RBCC_` families (leading-space comments so no line opens with hash):
  
  ```
    RBCC_role_governor="rbnae_governor"      # minted enum — RBRA_ROLE domain (3 members)
    RBCC_role_retriever="rbnae_retriever"
    RBCC_role_director="rbnae_director"
  
    RBCC_account_governor="governor"         # bare composition — SA-name + secret-dir (6 members)
    RBCC_account_retriever="retriever"
    RBCC_account_director="director"
    RBCC_account_payor="payor"
    RBCC_account_assay="assay"
    RBCC_account_mason="mason"
  ```
  
  `assay`/`mason`/`payor` are NEVER `RBRA_ROLE` values (validation accepts only
  governor|retriever|director), so they get NO `rbnae_` token — `account`-only.
  
  **Design A — secret directories stay bare** (operator-approved): a secret-dir name is a
  derived resource-name string in local-filesystem space, structurally identical to the SA
  email and the `-tether`/`-airgap` pool suffixes the heat already keeps bare under class-4.
  So directories follow `RBCC_account_*`; they are NOT renamed. The folio→file `case` in
  `zrbra_resolve_role` is the translation point that lets the folio mint while the directory
  stays bare. The prior "operators rename dirs by hand" / "have dirs been renamed?" breadcrumb
  and the flat→nested migration loop are VOID — retire them (section D).
  
  ## Why the split is safe (already-shaped seams — verified)
  - `zrbgg_create_service_account_with_key(account_name, …, role)` already takes
    account-name ($1, builds the email) and role ($4, writes RBRA_ROLE) as separate params.
    Split = feed $1 from `RBCC_account_*`, $4 from `RBCC_role_*`.
  - `zrbra_resolve_role` (rbra_cli.sh) maps folio → `RBDC_*_RBRA_FILE` *variable* via a
    `case`, so the folio string is decoupled from the directory name.
  - Swizzle guard (rbra_cli.sh) ties only `RBRA_ROLE == BUZ_FOLIO`; both mint together.
  
  ## Edit map — by axis
  All file:line refs verified against the tree at slate time.
  
  ### A. Declarations — rbcc_Constants.sh
  - :59-64 — replace the 6-member `RBCC_role_*` block with the two families above.
  - :94-95 — `RBCC_fact_ext_roster_*` compose fact-FILE names → use `RBCC_account_*`
    (keeps fact filenames `roster-retriever` bare; minting here would corrupt filenames).
  - :101 — rewrite the "Distinct from the credential RBCC_role_* family" axis comment to
    describe the role(enum)/account(label) split.
  
  ### B. Minted (enum-value axis → `RBCC_role_*` = `rbnae_*`)
  - rbgg_Governor.sh:592, :643 — invest $4 (becomes RBRA_ROLE).
  - rbgp_Payor.sh:1876 — `printf 'RBRA_ROLE=%s' "${RBCC_role_governor}"`.
  - rbra_regime.sh:69-71 — validation `case` arms → `rbnae_governor|rbnae_retriever|rbnae_director)`.
  - rbra_regime.sh:41 — enroll help string text → new values (each ≤20 chars; `rbnae_retriever`
    is 15, within `buv_string_enroll RBRA_ROLE 1 20`).
  - rbra_cli.sh:39-44 — `zrbra_resolve_role` case arms → `rbnae_*)`; RBDC var targets unchanged.
  - rbra_cli.sh:83 — `z_roles=(...)` display array → `rbnae_*` (labels the enum).
  - rbra_cli.sh:35,43 — "role required / Unknown role" die-text → reflect minted values.
  - **Tools/buk/buts/butcrg_RegimeCredentials.sh:36,48-49** — `z_roles=("governor"
    "retriever" "director")` is passed as the **folio** to `rbw-rav`/`rbw-rar` (:48-49).
    The folio mints, so `z_roles` must become `rbnae_*` or `zrbra_resolve_role` dies
    "Unknown RBRA role". CAUTION: this site is caught by NONE of the build/fast/grep
    gates — it is a workstation-only credential test, and the grep audit reads its bare
    `governor` as an acceptable class-C survivor. Silent breakage; this edit plus the
    required butcrg run (acceptance gate) are the only things that catch it.
  
  ### C. Bare (composition-label axis → `RBCC_account_*`)
  - rbgg_Governor.sh:560,569 — roster first arg (SA-name prefix match).
  - rbgg_Governor.sh:578,583,629,634,825,830,839,844 — `z_account_name`, divest first arg,
    and the "composes X-<identity>" doc strings.
  - rbgd_DepotConstants.sh:61 — `RBGD_MASON_EMAIL`.
  - rbgp_Payor.sh:1226,1622 — mason name/email; :1412,1744 — `== ${RBCC_role_governor}-*`
    SA-email match; :1772 — governor account-id.
  - Secret-dir paths (Design A, bare): rbdc_DerivedConstants.sh:35-39 (mkdir), :74-78
    (`RBDC_*_RBRA_FILE`); rblm_cli.sh:88,125,167; rbho path-checks rbhodb_director_bind.sh:49,
    rbhoda_director_airgap.sh:52, rbhodf_director_first_build.sh:40, rbhodg_director_graft.sh:51.
  
  ### C-bis. Explicitly left bare — NO edit (judgment recorded so the executor need not re-derive)
  - `rbgv_AccessProbe.sh` `zrbgv_role_rbra_file_capture` (~:76-95) has its OWN
    `governor)|director)|retriever)` role→file `case` plus internal bare labels in
    `rbgv_check_*`. These are bare-axis (file resolution + internal labels, structurally
    the directory) and STAY bare. They work post-split only because the probe sources the
    RBRA file and runs `zrbra_enforce`, whose case is minted in section B — confirm that
    coupling holds, then leave rbgv untouched.
  - Pre-existing bare `governor-*` literals NOT sourced from `RBCC_role_*`, all intentionally
    bare and untouched: rbgp_Payor.sh:1687 (doc prose), :1712 (step message), :1811
    (`"governor-owner"` IAM request-id label). The grep audit will surface these — leave them.
  
  ### D. Retire (no-auto-repair posture; Design A voids these)
  - rbdc_DerivedConstants.sh:42-71 — delete the flat→nested one-shot migration (both the
    role loop :42-61 and the payor `rbro.env` block :63-71). It is dead auto-repair.
    Post-split it goes INCOHERENT, not merely stale: :44/:46 build the dir path from
    `RBCC_role_*` (now `rbnae_governor/…`) while the real secret dirs stay bare — a path
    mismatch — so it cannot be mechanically repointed. Delete, do not repair. Drop the
    rename breadcrumb entirely — under Design A nothing renames.
  
  ### E. The one mixed-arg seam — split `zrbho_credential_install`
  - rbhob_base.sh:88 `zrbho_credential_install(z_role_constant)` uses its single arg as BOTH
    the displayed dir path (:97, :115 — needs bare) AND the folio passed to `rbw-rav`
    (:135 — needs minted). Give it two params: `(z_account_label, z_role_folio)`.
    - :97,:115 dir-path → `z_account_label` (bare).
    - :135 `buh_tt … "${RBZ_VALIDATE_AUTH}" "" " ${z_role_folio}"` → minted folio.
    - Callers: rbhocr_credential_retriever.sh:38, rbhocd_credential_director.sh:38 — pass
      `"${RBCC_account_X}" "${RBCC_role_X}"`.
  
  ### F. Rust projection + consumers (value-preserving rename)
  - rbcc_Constants.sh:126-134 — the `rbcc_emit_consts` projection list currently emits
    `RBCC_role_*` (6) → `RBTDGC_ROLE_*`. Rust uses these ONLY as secret-dir names
    (rbtdrp_pristine.rs:288 `secrets.join(role)`), i.e. the bare axis. Repoint the list to
    the 6 `RBCC_account_*`; projected names become `RBTDGC_ACCOUNT_*` (values unchanged).
  - rbcc_Constants.sh:110-119 — the projection-block comment ("credential roles", "the
    manifest role mirror") describes the projected set; refresh that prose to "account
    labels" so it matches the repointed list.
  - Discovery: `grep -rn 'RBTDGC_ROLE_' Tools/rbk/rbtd/src` — repoint every hit
    (rbtdrp_pristine `RBTDRP_RBRA_ROLES` = GOVERNOR/DIRECTOR/RETRIEVER/ASSAY, plus any
    rbtdrk/rbtdtm_manifest/lib.rs mirror) to `RBTDGC_ACCOUNT_*`.
  - Also `grep -rn 'RBRA_ROLE' Tools/rbk/rbtd/src` — IF any Rust fixture asserts an RBRA_ROLE
    *value* (not a dir path), it needs the minted value; then ALSO project `RBCC_role_*` as
    `RBTDGC_ROLE_*` and point only those value-assertions at it. (Verified at slate time:
    none — current Rust role use is `secrets.join(role)` dir-path only.)
  - Rebuild: `tt/vow-b.Build.sh`; unit tests: `tt/rbw-tt.Test.sh`.
  
  ### G. Spec text
  - BUS0 (Tools/buk/vov_veiled/BUS0-BashUtilitiesSpec.adoc) "Target Enums" (~:1025-1040) —
    add `Auth regime (rbnae_): RBRA_ROLE — governor/retriever/director → rbnae_*`, and a one-
    line note that auth-role uniquely splits into a minted enum family (`RBCC_role_*`,
    RBRA_ROLE domain) and a bare composition family (`RBCC_account_*`, SA-names + secret
    dirs), the bare side justified by class-4.
  - adoc account-name prose carrying `${RBCC_role_*}-` (RBSRL-retriever_roster:39,48,
    RBSRK-retriever_invest:15,33, RBSDK-director_invest:12,30, RBSDR-director_roster:37,45) —
    these describe the SA `accountId` prefix → switch the attribute ref to `RBCC_account_*`.
  
  ## Critical test caveat — the enum gate does NOT cover roles
  `RBRA_ROLE` is `buv_string_enroll` + a hand-written `case`, NOT `buv_enum_enroll`. So the
  paddock's "regime-validation rejects a bare survivor once the declaration flips" safety net
  does NOT apply here. The per-family grep audit AND the required butcrg run (below) are
  therefore LOAD-BEARING, not confirmatory — butcrg is the only automated exercise of the
  minted folio through `rbw-rav`/`rbw-rar`.
  
  ## Acceptance gate
  - `tt/vow-b.Build.sh` clean (deny-warnings) + `tt/rbw-tt.Test.sh` green.
  - `tt/rbw-ts.TestSuite.fast.sh` green (read ../logs-buk/last.txt; never pipe the tabtarget).
  - shellcheck via release-qualify path if touched broadly.
  - Grep audit: `grep -rn 'rbnae_governor\|rbnae_retriever\|rbnae_director' Tools/` returns a
    coherent enum-value cluster (RBRA_ROLE writes/validation/folio/resolver/display + the
    butcrg test folio only); NO `rbnae_*` leaks into an SA-name, email, or directory path.
    Bare `governor`/`director`/`retriever` survivors must all be: class-3 (colophons rbw-ac*,
    function names, doc filenames), rbgv resolver labels (C-bis), the rbgp prose/label
    literals (C-bis: :1687/:1712/:1811), or class-C account/dir composition.
  - **Required credential proof (replaces the former "optional" leg) — the mint is only
    proven end-to-end against real credentials, so close this gap rather than waving it:**
    - **Workstation, always:** run the `butcrg_RegimeCredentials.sh` RBRA cases (find its
      enrolled dispatch) — green proves the minted folio resolves through `rbw-rav`/`rbw-rar`
      and the swizzle guard. Also run `rbw-rav rbnae_<role>` for each role whose RBRA file is
      present.
    - **If GCP creds present:** a fresh invest (`rbw-aM` governor / `rbw-arI` retriever /
      `rbw-adI` director) writes `RBRA_ROLE=rbnae_*`, then the matching access-probe
      (`rbw-acg`/`rbw-acr`/`rbw-acd`) sources it and reaches GCP — exercising rbgv resolution
      + `zrbra_enforce` against the minted value.
    - **If creds for any role are absent:** state so explicitly in the wrap summary — name the
      untested leg. No silent skip.
  
  ## What done looks like
  Two `RBCC_` families exist; `RBRA_ROLE`/folio/validation/resolver/butcrg-test carry `rbnae_*`;
  every SA email/account-id and every secret-directory name is byte-identical to before; the
  flat→nested migration loop and rename breadcrumb are gone; rbgv and the rbgp prose/label
  literals left bare by design; BUS0 lists `rbnae_`; Rust projects `RBTDGC_ACCOUNT_*` and
  builds/tests green; the required credential proof is run (or its absent legs named in the wrap).

content.content:
1	# jjezs_paddock ₣BM
2	
3	## Context
4	
5	Regime enums in this project carry their values as bare English words —
6	`conjure`, `bind`, `graft` for `RBRV_VESSEL_MODE`; `tether`, `airgap` for
7	`RBRV_EGRESS_MODE`; node-regime modes (`disabled`/`enabled`/`global`/
8	`allowlist`) for the `RBRN_*` family. These bare values appear scattered
9	across bash, AsciiDoc, Python (Cloud Build steps), and Rust (test
10	fixtures), with no cluster-discoverable marker tying any one value to its
11	enum family. This makes mode renames cross-language hand-search-and-replace
12	work, prone to silent drift.
13	
14	There is one prior in-tree exemplar of the prefixed-value approach:
15	`bubep_linux | bubep_mac | bubep_windows` for `BURN_PLATFORM`. It works but
16	uses a different shape from what this heat adopts; harmonization is part of
17	the sweep.
18	
19	The inventory pace has been completed (see heat log / slate commit
20	messages for depth). This paddock records the resolved scope and the
21	durable findings; line-numbered site lists deliberately live in the
22	discovery recipe, not here.
23	
24	## MCM grounding — "sprue" is `mcm_sprue`, prefix discipline makes it an inlay
25	
26	"Sprue" here is not a coined word — it is the MCM quoin `mcm_sprue`: *a
27	literal wire-level token representing a serialized name — the exact
28	character sequence as it appears in a wire format (a JSON property key, an
29	API field name, a protocol identifier).* Our regime enum values are
30	textbook sprues: `rbnve_conjure` is the verbatim token that appears in
31	`rbrv.env`, in the `_RBGA_VESSEL_MODE` Cloud Build substitution, in
32	build-info JSON, and in Python/shell comparisons.
33	
34	MCM also states: *a sprue that follows the prefix naming discipline is also
35	an `mcm_inlay`* (prefix-recognized, not lexicon-catalogued). So precisely,
36	the values are *already* sprues even while bare; what this heat adds is the
37	prefix discipline that makes each sprue **also an inlay**. The spec pace
38	(₢BMAAA) should cite `mcm_sprue` + `mcm_inlay` for grounding rather than
39	inventing rationale.
40	
41	Note on a sibling usage: ₣BK independently uses "sprue" for tabtarget
42	dispatch tokens (`{owner}ml_{launcher-id}`, e.g. `rbml_rbw`) and wrote a
43	local gloss in BCG/CLAUDE.md. That is *also* a valid `mcm_sprue` instance
44	(a literal dispatch-wire token) — not a competing definition, a sibling
45	one. Both heats correctly name wire-level literal tokens "sprue." ₣BK's BCG
46	text would ideally cite `mcm_sprue` rather than read as a freshly-minted
47	concept, but that is ₣BK's documentation concern, not a blocker here.
48	
49	## Convention — locked
50	
51	Pattern: `<proj><n><regime><e>_<value>` where:
52	
53	- `<proj>` — project prefix (`rb` for Recipe Bottle, `bu` for BUK)
54	- `<n>`    — single letter signaling "this is a regime namespace value"
55	             (chosen `n` for "namespace", contrasting `r` which is taken
56	             for the regime-variable family `rbr*`/`bur*`)
57	- `<regime>` — single letter mirroring the regime family
58	             (`v` = vessel, `n` = node, `c` = config, `s` = station,
59	             `e` = environment, etc.)
60	- `<e>`    — single letter signaling "this is an enum value"
61	- `_<value>` — the canonical value name
62	
63	Full target table (all in-scope enums):
64	
65	| Variable                 | Regime | Bare → Sprue                                          |
66	|--------------------------|:------:|-------------------------------------------------------|
67	| RBRV_VESSEL_MODE         | v      | bind/conjure/graft → `rbnve_bind`/`_conjure`/`_graft` |
68	| RBRV_EGRESS_MODE         | v      | tether/airgap → `rbnve_tether`/`_airgap`              |
69	| RBRN_ENTRY_MODE          | n      | disabled/enabled → `rbnne_disabled`/`_enabled`        |
70	| RBRN_UPLINK_DNS_MODE     | n      | disabled/global/allowlist → `rbnne_*`                 |
71	| RBRN_UPLINK_ACCESS_MODE  | n      | disabled/global/allowlist → `rbnne_*`                 |
72	| BURN_PLATFORM            | n      | bubep_linux/mac/windows → `bunne_linux`/`_mac`/`_windows` |
73	
74	Family-sharing is a deliberate property, not an accident: vessel-mode and
75	egress-mode share `rbnve_` (both vessel-regime); the three node-mode enums
76	share `rbnne_`, so `rbnne_disabled` is intentionally voiced by ENTRY,
77	DNS, and ACCESS alike. Uniqueness lives in the full quoin + the owning
78	variable, not in the 5-char prefix. A single grep per family returns the
79	whole cluster.
80	
81	## Scope decisions — resolved
82	
83	- **RBRV_VESSEL_MODE + RBRV_EGRESS_MODE** → `rbnve_*`. In.
84	- **RBRN_ENTRY_MODE / UPLINK_DNS_MODE / UPLINK_ACCESS_MODE** → `rbnne_*`.
85	  In. Their values are pure comparison tokens with no second life, so the
86	  prefix is free (no indirection needed).
87	- **BURN_PLATFORM** → harmonize `bubep_*` to `bunne_*` in the same sweep.
88	  Decided harmonize over grandfather, per load-bearing-complexity
89	  discipline — eliminates the two-shape wart rather than freezing it.
90	- **RBRN_RUNTIME (`docker`/`podman`) — EXCLUDED, stays bare.** Its value
91	  is consumed as the literal executable name (`command -v "${RBRN_RUNTIME}"`,
92	  `${RBRN_RUNTIME} image inspect`). Adopting the convention would require a
93	  permanent value→binary indirection layer at every dispatch site — a
94	  runtime cost paid for grep-discoverability of two values that
95	  `RBRN_RUNTIME` itself already marks. The convention would cost more than
96	  it returns. This is the one regime enum that does not earn the prefix;
97	  the exclusion is deliberate, not an oversight.
98	- **BURE_VERBOSE / BURE_COLOR** — out (bare ints, different problem class;
99	  unchanged from original framing).
100	
101	## Comment / use-site classification — judgment work
102	
103	Bare-word grep on a value yields four classes. The migration paces walk
104	the candidate list and classify each hit:
105	
106	1. **Phase / concept references** — "the conjure phase", "the airgap
107	   build" — refer to the workflow, not the enum value. **Leave alone.**
108	2. **Enum-value references** — equality tests, case arms, JSON literals,
109	   `vessel mode is conjure`, defaults — the actual variable value.
110	   **Migrate.**
111	3. **Identifier embeds** — `RBSAC-ark_conjure.adoc`, `rbho_conjure_*`,
112	   vessel sigils `rbev-bottle-ifrit-airgap`, test-step labels like
113	   `ordain-conjure` — the bare word is part of an identifier. **Leave
114	   alone.**
115	4. **Coupled derived strings** — the value drives a derived token that
116	   lives in resource-name space and must stay bare even though it is
117	   semantically the enum value. **Leave the derived string bare; migrate
118	   only the dispatch that selects it.** Two confirmed witnesses:
119	   - egress → worker-pool suffixes (`RBGC_POOL_SUFFIX_*`, the `-tether`/
120	     `-airgap` fragments in pool paths)
121	   - vessel-mode → hallmark prefixes (`RBGC_HALLMARK_PREFIX_*` = `c`/`b`/`g`)
122	   Because of class 4, even a class-2 case arm is not blind find/replace:
123	   the arm becomes `rbnve_tether)` while the pool string it maps to stays
124	   `-tether`.
125	
126	### Three judgment traps a naive grep misses
127	
128	- **Default-value defaults** — `${z_mode:-conjure}` is class-2 but a
129	  `== "conjure"` grep skips it. The recipe must also grep `:-conjure`,
130	  `:-bind`, etc.
131	- **AsciiDoc wire-value tables read like prose** — a spec table cell
132	  documenting `(conjure, bind, or graft)` as a substitution value is
133	  class-2 (migrate), not class-1 prose. Easy to misclassify and leave
134	  stale.
135	- **Rust noise is almost all class-3** — the onboarding-scenario fixtures
136	  are full of step labels (`ordain-conjure`, `wrest-bind`) and sigil
137	  consts that stay bare. Only the `export RBRV_*_MODE="..."` baseline
138	  strings in the fast-fixture file actually migrate; that pace rebuilds
139	  the Rust crate (`tt/vow-b`).
140	
141	### Noise ranking (classification effort)
142	
143	Counterintuitively `conjure` is the noisiest value (heavy phase/operation
144	prose), then `bind` (English-word noise: bind-mount, IP bind), then
145	`graft`; `tether`/`airgap` are the cleanest (~10 sites each, mostly
146	class-2 or class-4). This is why egress is the cheap pilot and vessel-mode
147	is the heavy classification grind.
148	
149	## Cloud Build detection gap — vessel-mode acceptance gate
150	
151	Vessel-mode's value crosses a substitution boundary
152	(`_RBGA_VESSEL_MODE` / `_RBGV_VESSEL_MODE`) into a **remote execution
153	context** — Cloud Build step shells and Python steps compare against the
154	literal value. The local `regime-validation` net catches the
155	declaration↔Rust-fixture lockstep, but it does **not** exercise the
156	remote-side comparisons. A missed step-shell or Python site fails silently
157	until an actual conjure/graft cloud build runs.
158	
159	**Consequence:** for the vessel-mode pace, "gauntlet green" is not
160	sufficient proof of done. Its acceptance gate is an actual **conjure +
161	graft cloud build** (the full ordain path), not just the fast/crucible
162	suites.
163	
164	## Discovery recipe
165	
166	- `grep -rn 'buv_enum_enroll' Tools/buk/ Tools/rbk/` — authoritative
167	  declarations; cross-walk `buv_gate_enroll` invocations to confirm no
168	  enum missed.
169	- Per value V: `grep -rn '\bV\b' Tools/ tt/` filtered by extension for
170	  code sites; also `grep -rn ':-V\b'` for default-value defaults.
171	- Comment/prose: `grep -rn '#.*\bV\b'` (bash/Python), `'//.*\bV\b'`
172	  (Rust), and scan `Tools/*/vov_veiled/*.adoc` for prose + wire-value
173	  tables.
174	- Substitution boundary: trace `_RBG[AV]_VESSEL_MODE` /
175	  `_RBG[AV]_EGRESS*` through `rbfd_*`/`rbfv_*` step assembly into the
176	  `rbgja*`/`rbgjv*` Python+shell steps.
177	
178	## Posture
179	
180	**Burn-bridges migration.** No backwards compatibility. Depots reform on
181	execution, vessels re-charge, regime files get edited in place. Operator
182	pre-flight: `rbw-MZ` before sweep, all crucibles quenched.
183	
184	**Deep-test stragglers.** `regime-validation`, `dockerfile-hygiene`, and
185	`gauntlet` catch any local bare-value site, because the enum-validation
186	gate rejects a non-prefixed value once the declaration is updated. The
187	remote Cloud Build comparisons are the exception — see the detection gap
188	above.
189	
190	## Heat-shape note — ₣BK relationship (BK coding landed)
191	
192	₣BK (moorings-cutover) swept essentially this heat's file surface
193	(`Tools/rbk/**/*.sh`, `rbtd/src/*.rs`, `RBS*.adoc`) — but for path literals,
194	not enum values. The two heats are **semantically orthogonal** (different
195	concerns, mostly different lines). ₣BK's *coding* has landed (BKAAI
196	wrapped); the prior file-surface-churn concern is now historical — the tree
197	is settled and the re-validation confirmed the enum-value sites survived
198	intact.
199	
200	What remains and matters:
201	- **Start gate:** ₣BK's two-platform gauntlet acceptance is still pending.
202	  Begin this heat only after ₣BK's gauntlet passes on linux + macos and any
203	  cutover bugfixes land — so a known-good cutover baseline exists and every
204	  ₣BM gauntlet signal is unambiguously about enums, not the cutover.
205	- **Residual churn risk:** only late ₣BK bugfixes from its gauntlet runs.
206	  Low; re-grep before editing if BK commits land meanwhile.
207	- The bubep→bunne harmonize touches BUK files (₣BK's other subtree). ₣BK's
208	  BUK code has landed, so no hard coordination remains — just awareness of
209	  any late bugfix churn there.
210	
211	## What done looks like
212	
213	- All in-scope bare enum values replaced with sprue-prefixed values across
214	  bash, AsciiDoc, Python (Cloud Build), and Rust; `RBRN_RUNTIME` left bare.
215	- `buv_enum_enroll` (and gated `buv_gate_enroll`) declarations updated to
216	  expect the new values; regime files (`rbrv.env`, `rbrn.env`, BURN
217	  profiles) updated in place.
218	- Class-4 derived strings (pool suffixes, hallmark prefixes) left bare;
219	  their selecting dispatch migrated.
220	- `regime-validation` + `dockerfile-hygiene` + `gauntlet` green.
221	- Vessel-mode additionally proven by a real conjure + graft cloud build.
222	- A single grep per family returns the complete cluster:
223	  `grep -rn rbnve_ Tools/`, `grep -rn rbnne_ Tools/`,
224	  `grep -rn bunne_ Tools/` — and `grep -rn bubep_` returns nothing.
225	
226	## Out of scope
227	
228	- Sprue convention applied to non-regime-enum domains (status codes, JSON
229	  wire-format keys, etc.) — separate heat.
230	- `RBRN_RUNTIME` (`docker`/`podman`) — executable-name coupling, excluded
231	  by decision above.
232	- `BURE_VERBOSE` / `BURE_COLOR` integer values — different problem class.
233	- Backwards-compatibility shims of any kind.
234	
235	## Pace shape (deferred to mount-time)
236	
237	Resolved cadence is per-variable-family, ordered cheap-pilot-first:
238	
239	1. Convention spec authoring — formalize the convention as a spec section,
240	   citing `mcm_sprue` + `mcm_inlay` for grounding, plus the class-4
241	   "coupled derived string stays bare" rule (two witnesses) and the
242	   `RBRN_RUNTIME` exclusion rationale.
243	2. Egress-mode pilot — `rbnve_tether`/`_airgap`. Smallest, cleanest;
244	   proves the mechanical pattern and the class-4 distinction.
245	3. Vessel-mode — `rbnve_bind`/`_conjure`/`_graft`. Heaviest; crosses
246	   bash/adoc/Python(Cloud Build)/Rust. Acceptance gate = real conjure +
247	   graft cloud build.
248	4. Node-mode — `rbnne_*` for ENTRY/DNS/ACCESS. High English-word noise,
249	   heavy classification.
250	5. bubep → bunne harmonize — BUK files; last slice.
251	6. Final regression sweep — gauntlet end-to-end + per-family grep audit.
252	
253	Slate as thin dockets at mount-time (depth in slate commit messages); do
254	not pre-cut detailed site lists from this paddock.
255	
256	# jjezs_pace ₢BMAAM
257	
258	## Character
259	Mechanical migration with a real axis-split at the seam: one bare `RBCC_role_*`
260	family that today serves three jobs gets cleaved into a minted enum family and a
261	bare composition family. Edits are localized and value-preserving on the bare side;
262	the judgment is resolved below. Imminent execution — every file:line below was
263	verified against the tree at slate time, and the operator has frozen this surface
264	(no commits land here before mount), so the refs are trustworthy as written.
265	
266	## Goal
267	Make the RBRA-role enum greppable as a sprue family (`rbnae_*`) WITHOUT touching any
268	Google Cloud service-account email, account-id, or local secret-directory name. A
269	deliberate partial cleanup: mint only the enum-value axis; leave every derived
270	resource-name string bare.
271	
272	## The decision (locked by operator) — `{role, account}` split, Design A
273	
274	The current single family `RBCC_role_*` conflates two axes:
275	- **Enum-value axis** — the `RBRA_ROLE` value: written into credential files, validated,
276	  carried as the auth-regime folio, swizzle-checked. Domain = {governor, retriever,
277	  director} only.
278	- **Composition-label axis** — bare fragments that build GCP SA account-ids/emails AND
279	  local secret-directory names. Domain = {governor, retriever, director, payor, assay,
280	  mason}.
281	
282	Cleave into two `RBCC_` families (leading-space comments so no line opens with hash):
283	
284	```
285	  RBCC_role_governor="rbnae_governor"      # minted enum — RBRA_ROLE domain (3 members)
286	  RBCC_role_retriever="rbnae_retriever"
287	  RBCC_role_director="rbnae_director"
288	
289	  RBCC_account_governor="governor"         # bare composition — SA-name + secret-dir (6 members)
290	  RBCC_account_retriever="retriever"
291	  RBCC_account_director="director"
292	  RBCC_account_payor="payor"
293	  RBCC_account_assay="assay"
294	  RBCC_account_mason="mason"
295	```
296	
297	`assay`/`mason`/`payor` are NEVER `RBRA_ROLE` values (validation accepts only
298	governor|retriever|director), so they get NO `rbnae_` token — `account`-only.
299	
300	**Design A — secret directories stay bare** (operator-approved): a secret-dir name is a
301	derived resource-name string in local-filesystem space, structurally identical to the SA
302	email and the `-tether`/`-airgap` pool suffixes the heat already keeps bare under class-4.
303	So directories follow `RBCC_account_*`; they are NOT renamed. The folio→file `case` in
304	`zrbra_resolve_role` is the translation point that lets the folio mint while the directory
305	stays bare. The prior "operators rename dirs by hand" / "have dirs been renamed?" breadcrumb
306	and the flat→nested migration loop are VOID — retire them (section D).
307	
308	## Why the split is safe (already-shaped seams — verified)
309	- `zrbgg_create_service_account_with_key(account_name, …, role)` already takes
310	  account-name ($1, builds the email) and role ($4, writes RBRA_ROLE) as separate params.
311	  Split = feed $1 from `RBCC_account_*`, $4 from `RBCC_role_*`.
312	- `zrbra_resolve_role` (rbra_cli.sh) maps folio → `RBDC_*_RBRA_FILE` *variable* via a
313	  `case`, so the folio string is decoupled from the directory name.
314	- Swizzle guard (rbra_cli.sh) ties only `RBRA_ROLE == BUZ_FOLIO`; both mint together.
315	
316	## Edit map — by axis
317	All file:line refs verified against the tree at slate time.
318	
319	### A. Declarations — rbcc_Constants.sh
320	- :59-64 — replace the 6-member `RBCC_role_*` block with the two families above.
321	- :94-95 — `RBCC_fact_ext_roster_*` compose fact-FILE names → use `RBCC_account_*`
322	  (keeps fact filenames `roster-retriever` bare; minting here would corrupt filenames).
323	- :101 — rewrite the "Distinct from the credential RBCC_role_* family" axis comment to
324	  describe the role(enum)/account(label) split.
325	
326	### B. Minted (enum-value axis → `RBCC_role_*` = `rbnae_*`)
327	- rbgg_Governor.sh:592, :643 — invest $4 (becomes RBRA_ROLE).
328	- rbgp_Payor.sh:1876 — `printf 'RBRA_ROLE=%s' "${RBCC_role_governor}"`.
329	- rbra_regime.sh:69-71 — validation `case` arms → `rbnae_governor|rbnae_retriever|rbnae_director)`.
330	- rbra_regime.sh:41 — enroll help string text → new values (each ≤20 chars; `rbnae_retriever`
331	  is 15, within `buv_string_enroll RBRA_ROLE 1 20`).
332	- rbra_cli.sh:39-44 — `zrbra_resolve_role` case arms → `rbnae_*)`; RBDC var targets unchanged.
333	- rbra_cli.sh:83 — `z_roles=(...)` display array → `rbnae_*` (labels the enum).
334	- rbra_cli.sh:35,43 — "role required / Unknown role" die-text → reflect minted values.
335	- **Tools/buk/buts/butcrg_RegimeCredentials.sh:36,48-49** — `z_roles=("governor"
336	  "retriever" "director")` is passed as the **folio** to `rbw-rav`/`rbw-rar` (:48-49).
337	  The folio mints, so `z_roles` must become `rbnae_*` or `zrbra_resolve_role` dies
338	  "Unknown RBRA role". CAUTION: this site is caught by NONE of the build/fast/grep
339	  gates — it is a workstation-only credential test, and the grep audit reads its bare
340	  `governor` as an acceptable class-C survivor. Silent breakage; this edit plus the
341	  required butcrg run (acceptance gate) are the only things that catch it.
342	
343	### C. Bare (composition-label axis → `RBCC_account_*`)
344	- rbgg_Governor.sh:560,569 — roster first arg (SA-name prefix match).
345	- rbgg_Governor.sh:578,583,629,634,825,830,839,844 — `z_account_name`, divest first arg,
346	  and the "composes X-<identity>" doc strings.
347	- rbgd_DepotConstants.sh:61 — `RBGD_MASON_EMAIL`.
348	- rbgp_Payor.sh:1226,1622 — mason name/email; :1412,1744 — `== ${RBCC_role_governor}-*`
349	  SA-email match; :1772 — governor account-id.
350	- Secret-dir paths (Design A, bare): rbdc_DerivedConstants.sh:35-39 (mkdir), :74-78
351	  (`RBDC_*_RBRA_FILE`); rblm_cli.sh:88,125,167; rbho path-checks rbhodb_director_bind.sh:49,
352	  rbhoda_director_airgap.sh:52, rbhodf_director_first_build.sh:40, rbhodg_director_graft.sh:51.
353	
354	### C-bis. Explicitly left bare — NO edit (judgment recorded so the executor need not re-derive)
355	- `rbgv_AccessProbe.sh` `zrbgv_role_rbra_file_capture` (~:76-95) has its OWN
356	  `governor)|director)|retriever)` role→file `case` plus internal bare labels in
357	  `rbgv_check_*`. These are bare-axis (file resolution + internal labels, structurally
358	  the directory) and STAY bare. They work post-split only because the probe sources the
359	  RBRA file and runs `zrbra_enforce`, whose case is minted in section B — confirm that
360	  coupling holds, then leave rbgv untouched.
361	- Pre-existing bare `governor-*` literals NOT sourced from `RBCC_role_*`, all intentionally
362	  bare and untouched: rbgp_Payor.sh:1687 (doc prose), :1712 (step message), :1811
363	  (`"governor-owner"` IAM request-id label). The grep audit will surface these — leave them.
364	
365	### D. Retire (no-auto-repair posture; Design A voids these)
366	- rbdc_DerivedConstants.sh:42-71 — delete the flat→nested one-shot migration (both the
367	  role loop :42-61 and the payor `rbro.env` block :63-71). It is dead auto-repair.
368	  Post-split it goes INCOHERENT, not merely stale: :44/:46 build the dir path from
369	  `RBCC_role_*` (now `rbnae_governor/…`) while the real secret dirs stay bare — a path
370	  mismatch — so it cannot be mechanically repointed. Delete, do not repair. Drop the
371	  rename breadcrumb entirely — under Design A nothing renames.
372	
373	### E. The one mixed-arg seam — split `zrbho_credential_install`
374	- rbhob_base.sh:88 `zrbho_credential_install(z_role_constant)` uses its single arg as BOTH
375	  the displayed dir path (:97, :115 — needs bare) AND the folio passed to `rbw-rav`
376	  (:135 — needs minted). Give it two params: `(z_account_label, z_role_folio)`.
377	  - :97,:115 dir-path → `z_account_label` (bare).
378	  - :135 `buh_tt … "${RBZ_VALIDATE_AUTH}" "" " ${z_role_folio}"` → minted folio.
379	  - Callers: rbhocr_credential_retriever.sh:38, rbhocd_credential_director.sh:38 — pass
380	    `"${RBCC_account_X}" "${RBCC_role_X}"`.
381	
382	### F. Rust projection + consumers (value-preserving rename)
383	- rbcc_Constants.sh:126-134 — the `rbcc_emit_consts` projection list currently emits
384	  `RBCC_role_*` (6) → `RBTDGC_ROLE_*`. Rust uses these ONLY as secret-dir names
385	  (rbtdrp_pristine.rs:288 `secrets.join(role)`), i.e. the bare axis. Repoint the list to
386	  the 6 `RBCC_account_*`; projected names become `RBTDGC_ACCOUNT_*` (values unchanged).
387	- rbcc_Constants.sh:110-119 — the projection-block comment ("credential roles", "the
388	  manifest role mirror") describes the projected set; refresh that prose to "account
389	  labels" so it matches the repointed list.
390	- Discovery: `grep -rn 'RBTDGC_ROLE_' Tools/rbk/rbtd/src` — repoint every hit
391	  (rbtdrp_pristine `RBTDRP_RBRA_ROLES` = GOVERNOR/DIRECTOR/RETRIEVER/ASSAY, plus any
392	  rbtdrk/rbtdtm_manifest/lib.rs mirror) to `RBTDGC_ACCOUNT_*`.
393	- Also `grep -rn 'RBRA_ROLE' Tools/rbk/rbtd/src` — IF any Rust fixture asserts an RBRA_ROLE
394	  *value* (not a dir path), it needs the minted value; then ALSO project `RBCC_role_*` as
395	  `RBTDGC_ROLE_*` and point only those value-assertions at it. (Verified at slate time:
396	  none — current Rust role use is `secrets.join(role)` dir-path only.)
397	- Rebuild: `tt/vow-b.Build.sh`; unit tests: `tt/rbw-tt.Test.sh`.
398	
399	### G. Spec text
400	- BUS0 (Tools/buk/vov_veiled/BUS0-BashUtilitiesSpec.adoc) "Target Enums" (~:1025-1040) —
401	  add `Auth regime (rbnae_): RBRA_ROLE — governor/retriever/director → rbnae_*`, and a one-
402	  line note that auth-role uniquely splits into a minted enum family (`RBCC_role_*`,
403	  RBRA_ROLE domain) and a bare composition family (`RBCC_account_*`, SA-names + secret
404	  dirs), the bare side justified by class-4.
405	- adoc account-name prose carrying `${RBCC_role_*}-` (RBSRL-retriever_roster:39,48,
406	  RBSRK-retriever_invest:15,33, RBSDK-director_invest:12,30, RBSDR-director_roster:37,45) —
407	  these describe the SA `accountId` prefix → switch the attribute ref to `RBCC_account_*`.
408	
409	## Critical test caveat — the enum gate does NOT cover roles
410	`RBRA_ROLE` is `buv_string_enroll` + a hand-written `case`, NOT `buv_enum_enroll`. So the
411	paddock's "regime-validation rejects a bare survivor once the declaration flips" safety net
412	does NOT apply here. The per-family grep audit AND the required butcrg run (below) are
413	therefore LOAD-BEARING, not confirmatory — butcrg is the only automated exercise of the
414	minted folio through `rbw-rav`/`rbw-rar`.
415	
416	## Acceptance gate
417	- `tt/vow-b.Build.sh` clean (deny-warnings) + `tt/rbw-tt.Test.sh` green.
418	- `tt/rbw-ts.TestSuite.fast.sh` green (read ../logs-buk/last.txt; never pipe the tabtarget).
419	- shellcheck via release-qualify path if touched broadly.
420	- Grep audit: `grep -rn 'rbnae_governor\|rbnae_retriever\|rbnae_director' Tools/` returns a
421	  coherent enum-value cluster (RBRA_ROLE writes/validation/folio/resolver/display + the
422	  butcrg test folio only); NO `rbnae_*` leaks into an SA-name, email, or directory path.
423	  Bare `governor`/`director`/`retriever` survivors must all be: class-3 (colophons rbw-ac*,
424	  function names, doc filenames), rbgv resolver labels (C-bis), the rbgp prose/label
425	  literals (C-bis: :1687/:1712/:1811), or class-C account/dir composition.
426	- **Required credential proof (replaces the former "optional" leg) — the mint is only
427	  proven end-to-end against real credentials, so close this gap rather than waving it:**
428	  - **Workstation, always:** run the `butcrg_RegimeCredentials.sh` RBRA cases (find its
429	    enrolled dispatch) — green proves the minted folio resolves through `rbw-rav`/`rbw-rar`
430	    and the swizzle guard. Also run `rbw-rav rbnae_<role>` for each role whose RBRA file is
431	    present.
432	  - **If GCP creds present:** a fresh invest (`rbw-aM` governor / `rbw-arI` retriever /
433	    `rbw-adI` director) writes `RBRA_ROLE=rbnae_*`, then the matching access-probe
434	    (`rbw-acg`/`rbw-acr`/`rbw-acd`) sources it and reaches GCP — exercising rbgv resolution
435	    + `zrbra_enforce` against the minted value.
436	  - **If creds for any role are absent:** state so explicitly in the wrap summary — name the
437	    untested leg. No silent skip.
438	
439	## What done looks like
440	Two `RBCC_` families exist; `RBRA_ROLE`/folio/validation/resolver/butcrg-test carry `rbnae_*`;
441	every SA email/account-id and every secret-directory name is byte-identical to before; the
442	flat→nested migration loop and rename breadcrumb are gone; rbgv and the rbgp prose/label
443	literals left bare by design; BUS0 lists `rbnae_`; Rust projects `RBTDGC_ACCOUNT_*` and
444	builds/tests green; the required credential proof is run (or its absent legs named in the wrap).
445
